mechanism_narrative: Near-zero federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive forward S&P 500 returns as capital flows into risk assets.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months when FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rates exceeding 9.5% signal persistent labor market slack that suppresses consumer spending expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >60% of triggered months despite UNRATE exceeding 9.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and builder confidence, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows when both series trend upward simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above 0.5% month-over-month during post-recession phases signals capacity utilization recovery, triggering positive short-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >50% of months with INDPRO MoM growth exceeding 0.5% during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI inflation exceeding 0.4% triggers expectations of premature monetary tightening, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day returns are positive in >55% of months when CPIAUCSL MoM exceeds 0.4%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls alongside falling unemployment rates confirm genuine labor market tightening, driving positive equity returns over a quarter as earnings expectations rise.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >50% of quarters where PAYEMS increases and UNRATE declines simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth below 1.0% annualized reflects weak aggregate demand that depresses corporate revenue forecasts, producing negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of quarters with GDPC1 annualized growth below 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index levels above 100 during post-crisis recovery phases indicate restored manufacturing capacity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: 20-day returns are negative in >55% of months when INDPRO exceeds 100 during documented recovery regimes.
